name: Aerosol Spray
description: Aerosol sprays such as deodorant, hairspray, and sunscreen are allowed in carry-on if containers are 3.4oz/100ml or less and fit in a quart-sized bag. In checked baggage, toiletry aerosols up to 18oz/500ml per container are allowed with a total limit of 70oz/2L.
tsaGuideline: Aerosol toiletries (deodorant, hairspray, shaving cream, etc.) are allowed in carry-on if each container is 3.4oz (100ml) or less and fits in your one quart-sized resealable bag. In checked baggage, each toiletry aerosol container must be 18oz (500ml) or less with a total aggregate of 70oz (2L) per passenger.
iataGuideline: IATA permits aerosols classified as toiletry or medicinal articles in checked baggage up to 500ml/500g per container, with a total net quantity not exceeding 2L or 2kg per person. Non-toiletry aerosols (like spray paint) are prohibited.
capacityLimit: Carry-on: 3.4oz (100ml) per container in quart bag. Checked: 18oz (500ml) per container, 70oz (2L) total per passenger.
restrictions: Carry-on containers must be 3.4oz/100ml or less
Must fit in one quart-sized resealable plastic bag for carry-on
Checked bags limited to 18oz/500ml per container
Total checked aerosol limit of 70oz/2L per passenger
Only toiletry/medicinal aerosols allowed; industrial aerosols prohibited
Release valve must be protected by a cap or other means to prevent accidental discharge

faqs.question: Can I bring aerosol deodorant in my carry-on?
faqs.answer: Yes, if the container is 3.4oz (100ml) or smaller and fits in your quart-sized liquids bag. Travel-sized aerosol deodorants are widely available and designed to meet this requirement.

faqs.question: Is there a limit on aerosol cans in checked luggage?
faqs.answer: Yes. Each toiletry aerosol container must be 18oz (500ml) or less, and the total combined quantity of all aerosols and other restricted toiletries cannot exceed 70oz (2L) per passenger.

faqs.question: Are all types of aerosol sprays allowed?
faqs.answer: No. Only personal care/toiletry aerosols (deodorant, hairspray, shaving cream, sunscreen) and medicinal aerosols are permitted. Industrial or flammable aerosols like spray paint, WD-40, or cooking spray are prohibited in both carry-on and checked bags.
